name: Bachelor of Science in Food Technology
college: College of Food and Nutrition and Dietetics (CFND)
overview: The BS Food Technology program trains students in the science of food processing, preservation, quality control, and product development. Students learn how food is produced safely, how its shelf life is extended, how new food products are created, and how food quality is maintained from farm to table. It is a science-based program with strong industry applications.
ideal_student: This program is ideal for students who are passionate about food from a scientific perspective — not just cooking, but understanding the chemistry and biology behind it. Students who enjoy laboratory work, are detail-oriented about quality and safety, and are interested in creating new food products or improving existing ones thrive here.
skills: Food chemistry, microbiology, food processing techniques, quality control, product development, sensory evaluation, food safety regulations, laboratory analysis, packaging technology, and HACCP implementation.
careers: Food technologist, quality assurance officer, food product developer, food safety inspector, production supervisor in food manufacturing, regulatory affairs specialist, food researcher, nutritionist-technologist, food entrepreneur.
work_environment: Graduates work in food manufacturing companies, processing plants, fast food chains, government food regulatory agencies (FDA, BFAD), research institutions, and their own food businesses. Work involves laboratory testing, production floor supervision, and product formulation.
strand_alignment: Recommended for students from STEM strand with strong chemistry and biology performance. Students from TVL-Home Economics with high science grades may also qualify.
keywords: food technology science chemistry biology laboratory food processing preservation safety quality control product development food manufacturing packaging shelf life bacteria contamination HACCP food safety regulations FDA food industry cooking baking ingredients nutrition food business snacks beverages production
holland_rationale: BSFT is primarily Investigative because it is fundamentally a science — food chemistry, microbiology, research-driven product development, and systematic quality analysis require deep analytical and scientific thinking. Realistic is secondary because food technologists work hands-on in laboratories, production floors, and processing facilities operating equipment and conducting physical tests.
